---
title: 'What is Towdah?'
date: '2013-03-24T21:23:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Towdah: tō·dä'''
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1942973699720403036'
blogger_filename: '/2013/03/what-is-towdah.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Towdah_ tō·dä_/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Insight'
---

<br />
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
Toda in spanish means: ALL</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
Towdaw in Hebrew means: Praise, Confession, and Thanksgiving</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
Therefore I am to give ALL Praise, Confession, and Thanksgiving to God.</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
I know I’m called to music, worship, and praise as a central part of my life. But I’ve been pushing it off and laying it aside for FAR too long. Over the years I’ve spent lot’s of time on and off Church Platforms doing Praise and Worship. It seems that just as soon as I get anywhere close to a platform I start feeling ill,&nbsp;agitated, sick, nervous, scared, and depressed. It’s happened every time at every church. At one time I thought as I got closer to the calling of God and the right church it would go away. Then I realized that it was harder the better the church I was in.</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
Some may be tempted to call this stage fright. It’s not. I don’t have stage fright. I don’t have any kind of fright. Fear isn’t allowed in my life. It’s an enemy to a child of God. I’ve been working to remove fear. But this is more…</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
I was confused for a long time about this. Then I realized in prayer and study over the years that the more power of God (also known as anointing) that was present on a church, platform, and Pastor the more my reaction grew instead of shrank!!</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
After many years of asking the Lord about this reaction I was having I finally just decided that I couldn’t be on a platform anymore until I knew what was happening. Several years went by and the music inside grew dry. I didn’t even like listening to it. I&nbsp;didn’t&nbsp;want to be present for the worship part of the service. I all bust forgot about music. During that time God started dealing with me about the level of my intimacy with Him. I was keeping Him at a distance… as I was everyone else too. Over the last few months He’s been teaching me about prayer and closeness to Him. I’ve grown at work. I even started developing a mindset that I didn’t know I could have and that was: To embrace and not reject leadership and authority!</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
Then, slowly… over the last few months, especially since 2010 started, He has brought music back to me. It started back around Sept during the Days of Refreshing during Rosh Hashanah.</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
I am now taking an active role in bringing music back into my life as of today. I will start with this website as a central resource center for everything and Learn and Do.</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>Confess-Praise-Thanksgiving… These are the heart of true music!</strong></div>
<h1 id="firstHeading" style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; line-height: 19px;">
<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosh_Hashanah" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosh_Hashanah" target="_blank">Rosh Hashanah</a></h1>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
תּוֹדָ</div>
<table border="0" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="0" class="mceItemTable" style="border: 1px dashed rgb(187, 187, 187); cursor: default; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; width: 100%px;"><tbody>
<tr><td style="border: 1px dashed rgb(187, 187, 187); cursor: text; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; margin: 8px;"><a data-mce-href="/lang/lexicon/Lexicon.cfm?strongs=H8426" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/lang/lexicon/Lexicon.cfm?strongs=H8426">H8426</a></td><td style="border: 1px dashed rgb(187, 187, 187); cursor: text; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; margin: 8px;"><em>towdah</em></td><td style="border: 1px dashed rgb(187, 187, 187); cursor: text; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; margin: 8px;">tō·dä’</td><td style="border: 1px dashed rgb(187, 187, 187); cursor: text; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; margin: 8px;">thanksgiving,&nbsp;praise, thanks, thank offerings, confession</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>1)</strong>&nbsp;confession, praise, thanksgiving</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>a)</strong>&nbsp;give praise to God</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>b)</strong>&nbsp;thanksgiving in songs of liturgical worship, hymn of praise</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>c)</strong>&nbsp;thanksgiving choir or procession or line or company</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>d)</strong>&nbsp;thank-offering, sacrifice of thanksgiving</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>e)</strong>&nbsp;confession</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<a data-mce-href="http://towdah.files.wordpress.com/2010/04/lexicon-for-towdah.jpg" href="http://towdah.files.wordpress.com/2010/04/lexicon-for-towdah.jpg"><img alt="" data-mce-src="http://towdah.files.wordpress.com/2010/04/lexicon-for-towdah.jpg?w=450&amp;h=265" height="265" src="http://towdah.files.wordpress.com/2010/04/lexicon-for-towdah.jpg?w=450&amp;h=265" style="border: 0px; cursor: default;" title="Lexicon for Towdah" width="450" /></a></div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
From&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="./Lexicon.cfm?Strongs=H3034&amp;t=KJV" href="http://towdah.wordpress.com/wp-admin/Lexicon.cfm?Strongs=H3034&amp;t=KJV" title="= praise, give thanks, confess, thank, make confession, thanksgiving, cast, cast out, shoot, thankful">יָדָה (H3034)</a></div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
From the Root Word:</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
יָדָה</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<em>yadah &nbsp; yä·dä’</em></div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>1)</strong>&nbsp;to throw, shoot, cast</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>a)</strong>&nbsp;(Qal) to shoot (arrows)</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>b)</strong>&nbsp;(Piel) to cast, cast down, throw down</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>c)</strong>&nbsp;(Hiphil)</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>1)</strong>&nbsp;to give thanks,, laud, praise</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>2)</strong>&nbsp;to confess, confess (the name of God)</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>d)</strong>&nbsp;(Hithpael)</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>1)</strong>&nbsp;to confess (sin)</div>
<div style="color: #333333; font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">
<strong>2)</strong>&nbsp;to give thanks</div>
